explanation: Every pentest should begin with passive reconnaissance (footprinting) that does not touch the target, before any active scanning like Nmap or OS fingerprinting.
The -sP (ping scan) option performs a simple ping sweep to discover live hosts without port scanning.

answerText: B. To secure user sessions by ensuring that requests are coming from the same user
explanation: Session tokens authenticate that subsequent requests belong to the same authenticated session, preventing hijacking/impersonation; the other options describe unrelated benefits.

answerText: B. These session ID names can be used to fingerprint the application framework employed.
explanation: Default session ID names (e.g., PHPSESSID, JSESSIONID, ASP.NET_SessionId) reveal which framework is in use, aiding attackers in framework-specific exploits.
Transport mode encrypts only the payload and is typically used for end-to-end (internal, host-to-host) communications, whereas Tunnel mode is used for gateway-to-gateway (site-to-site) traffic.

answerText: A. C (and C. C++ also apply)
explanation: Buffer overflows arise from unsafe manual memory/pointer handling, which is characteristic of C and C++; Perl and Java manage memory/bounds automatically and are far less prone to classic overflow exploits.
Stealth (SYN/half-open, FIN, NULL, XMAS) scans avoid completing the TCP handshake and are crafted to slip past simple firewall/IDS rules that only watch for full connections.

answerText: C. Users can disable or circumvent updates just as they can if their computer is configured to use the vendor's online update service.
explanation: This describes a persisting limitation/risk, not a benefit, of using an automated patch management service â€” the other options are genuine advantages (central approval, bandwidth savings, automatic hotfix deployment).

answerText: B. Verifying and cleaning data from users to prevent exploits.
explanation: Input sanitization means validating and stripping/escaping malicious or malformed data (e.g., against SQL injection, XSS) before it is processed.
DumpSec is a dedicated Windows enumeration tool that dumps permissions, shares, users, and policies from Windows systems, unlike Nmap (scanning), Nessus (vulnerability scanning), or SNMPwalk (SNMP).

answerText: B. To replicate itself and spread to other systems
explanation: By definition, a computer virus's core purpose is self-replication and propagation, attaching itself to files/programs to spread.
Application whitelisting only permits known, approved, and verified executables; an unidentified/unknown executable is exactly what whitelisting is designed to block.

answerText: B. Direct interaction with system hardware
explanation: C is a low-level, compiled language that allows direct memory and hardware access, which is why it's widely used in systems/security programming (and also why it is prone to buffer overflow vulnerabilities).

answerText: A. caching of HTTP messages (and D. enabling HTTP transfers across a firewall)
explanation: Web proxies commonly cache HTTP responses to improve performance and act as an intermediary that allows HTTP traffic through a firewall on behalf of internal clients.

answerText: D. Zombies, compromised computers, are unaware that their systems are sending malicious packets.
explanation: DDoS traffic originates from many innocent, compromised zombie hosts whose unwitting owners have no idea they are participating, making it hard to trace and block a single source, unlike a DoS attack which has one identifiable origin.

answerText: B. Unauthorized access to or theft of information from a Bluetooth-enabled device
explanation: Bluesnarfing is the theft of data (contacts, messages, files) from a Bluetooth device via an unauthorized connection, as opposed to bluejacking which just sends unsolicited messages.

In CEH wireless-attack taxonomy, a honeypot/honeyspot AP is an attacker-configured access point that mimics a legitimate public hotspot (e.g., the McDonald's Wi-Fi) with a strong signal to lure users into connecting, so it can capture their credentials. A 'rogue access point' instead refers to an unauthorized AP attached to an organization's own internal network, which does not fit a public-hotspot scenario like Joe's.

answerText: B. It sends alerts when traffic deviates from a baseline
explanation: Anomaly-based IDS builds a baseline of normal behavior and raises alerts when observed traffic significantly deviates from it, rather than matching known signatures or blocking traffic itself.
A MITM attack involves an attacker secretly intercepting and possibly altering communication between two parties who believe they are communicating directly with each other.

answerText: C. Monitoring equipment in large-scale industries
explanation: SCADA (Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition) systems monitor and control industrial processes and equipment such as power plants, water treatment, and manufacturing.

answerText: A. Security update checks (and B. IIS checks)
explanation: Microsoft Baseline Security Analyzer (MBSA) checks for missing security updates/patches and reviews configuration issues such as IIS settings; it does not do hardware or virtualization checks.

answerText: A. The extra packets required may trigger an IDS alarm.
explanation: A TCP Connect scan completes the full three-way handshake (extra SYN-ACK-ACK-RST packets) for every port, generating more traffic/log entries that are far more likely to trigger IDS alerts than a stealthy SYN scan.
Subnetting segments a network into smaller broadcast domains, limiting exposure and making it harder for an attacker to move laterally across the entire network, improving security.

answerText: C. Both knowledge of network and computer technology and the ability to communicate with management and IT personnel.
explanation: Successful security professionals need strong technical skills as well as the soft skill of clearly communicating findings and risk to both management and technical staff.

To pull data back out of an NTFS Alternate Data Stream (file1.txt:hidden.exe), you read the stream with cat and redirect it out to a normal file; option C instead writes/creates a stream, and B and D use invalid syntax.
Spear phishing is a targeted social-engineering email attack aimed at specific individuals/organizations, often combined with exploit payloads, as opposed to generic mass email phishing.
Google, NetworkSolutions/Namedroppers-type WHOIS lookups, Sam Spade, and ARIN are all commonly used, legitimate OSINT sources for gathering domain and IP registration information.
NIST selected the Rijndael cipher, developed by Daemen and Rijmen, as the Advanced Encryption Standard (AES) in 2001, over finalists Serpent, Twofish, and RC6.

answerText: C. A security contractor accesses computers that have classified information without authorization.
explanation: The Stored Wire and Electronic Communications Act (18 U.S.C. Chapter 121) criminalizes unauthorized access to stored electronic communications/systems, which matches unauthorized access by a contractor; the other options involve authorized access or fall under different statutes (e.g., copyright, Economic Espionage Act).
Scripting in cybersecurity (Perl, Python, Bash, PowerShell, etc.) is primarily used to automate repetitive tasks such as scanning, log analysis, and exploit chaining.
Embedded operating systems are notoriously difficult to patch â€” updates are infrequent, vendor-dependent, and often require physical access or downtime, making patching a major security challenge.
Perl is widely used by system/network administrators for text processing, automation, and network scripting tasks, including many security tools.

answerText: A. identifying the applications and services on the client and server that should handle transmitted data (also C and F apply)
explanation: The transport layer (TCP/UDP) is responsible for identifying application/service ports, meeting reliability requirements, and multiplexing multiple conversations over one network â€” packet routing (D) and framing/error-checking of frames (B) belong to the network and data-link layers respectively.
X-ray is an Android vulnerability scanner app that checks a device for unpatched vulnerabilities, unlike evasi0n7/Pangu (iOS jailbreak tools) or DroidSheep Guard (session-hijacking protection).
Network Address Translation (NAT) maps one IP address (typically private/internal) to another (public) address on a firewall/router, hiding internal addressing from external networks.

answerText: B. User Security Identifiers in Windows environments
explanation: SID enumeration targets Windows Security Identifiers to discover valid user and group accounts on a system or domain.
A Network Intrusion Detection System (NIDS) passively monitors traffic and alerts administrators to malicious activity but does not itself block traffic, unlike a NIPS which actively prevents it.
WPA (Wi-Fi Protected Access) was introduced as an interim replacement to address WEP's serious cryptographic weaknesses, later followed by WPA2 and WPA3.
Enumeration reveals logical/network information such as services, user accounts, ports, and shares, but not physical hardware details.

answerText: A. IPv4 addresses use 32 bits represented in decimal format, while IPv6 addresses are 128 bits and written in hexadecimal format, providing a larger address space and better security.
explanation: This correctly describes the core technical difference: IPv4 is a 32-bit dotted-decimal address, IPv6 is a 128-bit hexadecimal address giving a vastly larger space and built-in support for features like IPSec; the other options reverse or misstate these facts.

answerText: A. retransmitting any unacknowledged data (and C. acknowledging received data)
explanation: TCP is connection-oriented and reliable, providing acknowledgments and retransmission of lost data, features UDP (a connectionless, best-effort protocol) does not provide.
Discarded documents can reveal passwords, meeting/vacation schedules useful for timing an intrusion, and manuals indicating the OS in use â€” all valid reasons dumpster diving is effective.
A sequence of instructions written to automate tasks on a system is a script, commonly used by penetration testers to streamline repetitive actions.

Network security specifically addresses protecting devices and infrastructure (routers, switches, firewalls) that make up the network, distinct from host/computer security which focuses on individual endpoints.
Fping is a command-line utility designed to ping multiple hosts in parallel, making it an efficient tool for performing ping sweeps.

answerText: D. Nondisclosure agreements assure clients that testers will not reveal or use any information they find.
explanation: An NDA legally binds the pentesters to confidentiality, reassuring management that any passwords or sensitive data discovered during testing will not be disclosed or misused.

answerText: B. To pass data between a web server and a web browser
explanation: CGI defines a standard way for a web server to execute external programs and pass data (e.g., form input) between the server and client/browser to generate dynamic content.

answerText: C. An unauthorized access point added to the network without the network administrator's consent
explanation: A rogue access point is any wireless AP installed on a secure network without explicit authorization from the network administrator, creating a backdoor into the network.

answerText: A. SNMPUtil (also B. SNScan and D. Solarwinds IP Network Browser)
explanation: SNMPUtil, SNScan, and Solarwinds IP Network Browser are all recognized SNMP enumeration tools; SNMPScan is not a real standard tool name and Nmap is a general-purpose scanner rather than a dedicated SNMP enumerator.

The nmap http-methods script flags methods like CONNECT (and TRACE, PUT, DELETE) as potentially risky because CONNECT can be abused to proxy arbitrary traffic through the server, unlike the safe, common GET/POST/HEAD methods.
